message:
fix(frankenterm-core/tests): redirect stale replay/fleet/tantivy imports to extracted sub-crates

After the ft-y0loj.{1,3,4} sub-crate splits (tantivy → frankenterm-core-tantivy,
fleet_dashboard → frankenterm-core-fleet, 24 replay_* modules →
frankenterm-core-replay), six integration tests and two benches in
frankenterm-core were left importing the moved modules from the old
`frankenterm_core::*` paths. The parent crate no longer re-exports those
modules, so `cargo check -p frankenterm-core --tests --benches` failed
before this commit.

Companion to 42adcc0e (which fixed the same class of break in the `ft`
binary). The binary touches only a handful of replay modules; this commit
covers the full set of test/bench breakage.

Changes:

- crates/frankenterm-core/Cargo.toml: add frankenterm-core-fleet to
  [dev-dependencies] alongside the existing frankenterm-core-tantivy and
  frankenterm-core-replay dev-deps. All three are dev-only edges; the
  parent crate's production dep graph is unchanged.

- 4 replay integration tests + 1 conformance test + 1 replay bench:
  rewrite `frankenterm_core::replay_<moved>::*` imports as
  `frankenterm_core_replay::replay_<moved>::*`. `replay_decision_graph`
  imports stay on the parent path because that module is re-exported via
  `pub use frankenterm_core_replay_types::replay_decision_graph;`.

- proptest_fleet_dashboard.rs: `frankenterm_core::fleet_dashboard::*` →
  `frankenterm_core_fleet::fleet_dashboard::*`.

- benches/tantivy_search.rs: `frankenterm_core::tantivy_{ingest,query}::*`
  → `frankenterm_core_tantivy::tantivy_{ingest,query}::*`.

- Cargo.lock: regenerated to register the new frankenterm-core-fleet
  dev-dep edge from frankenterm-core.

Verified via:

  rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-orchreview-target \\
    CC=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang \\
    CXX=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang++ \\
    cargo check -p frankenterm-core --tests --benches

Finishes clean (0 errors, pre-existing warnings only). Workspace lib+bins
build still clean. ft-y378j.3 runtime_compat audit guard still clean (45
references across allowlisted files).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
